commit.message:
feat: add opencode go and opencode zen provider support. (#28)

This pull request adds support for two new OpenCode providers—OpenCode
Zen and OpenCode Go—enabling access to a wide variety of language models
through the OpenCode API. The integration includes provider definitions,
model lists, and context window settings, and ensures proper API
compatibility. Additionally, the necessary SDK dependency is added.

**New Provider Integrations:**

* Added the `opencodeZen` and `opencodeGo` providers to the codebase,
making them available in the built-in provider list and export surface
of `src/core/llm/providers/index.ts`.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-ddb28c3c24bb56042cb311eac77242dbe9e5094327453f853a305037f7b4eab9R17-R18)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-ddb28c3c24bb56042cb311eac77242dbe9e5094327453f853a305037f7b4eab9R41-R42)
[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-ddb28c3c24bb56042cb311eac77242dbe9e5094327453f853a305037f7b4eab9R67-R68)
* Implemented `src/core/llm/providers/opencode-zen.ts`, defining the
OpenCode Zen provider with support for a large set of models (GPT,
Claude, Gemini, etc.), dynamic model fetching, and detailed context
window settings.
* Implemented `src/core/llm/providers/opencode-go.ts`, defining the
OpenCode Go provider with a curated list of fallback models (GLM, Kimi,
MiMo, MiniMax) and context window settings.

**Dependency Updates:**

* Added the `@ai-sdk/openai-compatible` dependency to `package.json` to
enable compatibility with OpenAI-style APIs required by the new
providers.

Fix JSON parsing: handle unescaped quotes in LLM code output

Add _fix_unescaped_quotes strategy to safe_parse_json that detects and
escapes double-quotes inside JSON string values that LLMs forget to
escape (e.g. replace(",", ".") where some quotes are escaped but
others are not).

Key insight: tracks key vs value position in JSON structure — keys are
trusted (simple identifiers), while value strings use structural
lookahead (requires ,"key": or } or ] after the closing quote) to
distinguish real string terminators from unescaped quotes in code.

Added 13 new tests for safe_parse_json covering: valid JSON, markdown
fences, invalid escapes, partial/full unescaped quotes, complex code
patterns, trailing commas, single-quoted keys, nested objects, and
the exact real-world failure from QA testing.

All 244 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: support multi-file KUBECONFIG with three-way merge (#970)

* fix: support multi-file KUBECONFIG with three-way merge (#969)

The three-way merge code path used a shallow copy of EnvSettings which
caused ConfigFlags.KubeConfig to point to the wrong struct. When
KUBECONFIG contained multiple colon-separated paths, the entire string
was passed as ExplicitPath to the k8s client loader, causing os.Stat()
to fail with 'no such file or directory'.

Fix by creating a fresh EnvSettings and clearing KubeConfig when it
contains multiple paths, allowing the default loading rules to handle
multi-file KUBECONFIG via the Precedence field.

Signed-off-by: yxxhero <aiopsclub@163.com>

* fix: remove unused envSettings global variable causing lint failure

commit.message:
fix: use SBE-generated PacketHeader (16 bytes) and FramingHeader for UMDF packet framing

Replace the incorrect hand-written UmdfPacketHeader (14 bytes with wrong field
layout) with the SBE-generated PacketHeader (16 bytes) and FramingHeader (4 bytes
per message) composites.

UMDF wire format:
  [PacketHeader 16B: channelNumber + reserved + sequenceVersion + sequenceNumber + sendingTime]
  [FramingHeader 4B: messageLength + encodingType][SBE message]
  [FramingHeader 4B: messageLength + encodingType][SBE message]
  ...

This fixes all message parsing — the app now successfully processes 52M packets,
parsing 18,380 instrument definitions, 15.7M orders, 1.1M trades across 3,639 books.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix WASM effects: Embind base class binding for process()

Root cause: EXPORT_WASM_EFFECT macro registered derived classes without
declaring the WASMEffectBase relationship. Embind silently dropped methods
defined only in the base class (process, isInitialized, getSampleRate,
getParameterName). The worklet called effect.process() which was undefined,
caught by try/catch and fell through to passthrough mode — ALL 6 WASM
effects were silently passing audio through unprocessed.

Fix: Register WASMEffectBase as a proper Embind base class with all methods,
then derive effect classes with emscripten::base<WASMEffectBase>. Also changed
processJS signature from uintptr_t to unsigned int for Embind type resolution.

Rebuilt all 6 affected WASM effects:
- SpringReverb (confirmed params now affect audio in Node.js test)
- MVerb
- Leslie
- MoogFilters
- ShimmerReverb
- GranularFreeze

Also:
- Removed temporary FX-DEBUG console.warn logs
- MasterEffectsPanel: replaced FULL_WET_EFFECTS with getDefaultEffectWet()
- fx-audit.ts: comprehensive rewrite with knob testing
